Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to create a Python dictionary representing a Log Skeleton for a specific order fulfillment process scenario This dictionary must adhere to a specific schema containing keys for various declarative constraints equivalence always before always after never together activ freq directly follows

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Scenario Context Large scale distribution center order fulfillment Activities Receive Order Check Availability Reserve Items Pick Items Quality Check Pack Order Generate Shipping Label Dispatch Invoice Customer Record Payment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine Constraints based on Business Logic I need to infer logical relationships between these activities to populate the dictionary keys Real world process knowledge is required here

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Sequence RO happens first CA must happen after RO RI must happen after CA PI must happen after RI QC must happen after PI PO must happen after QC GSL usually happens around packing or just before dispatch Lets say before Dispatch D happens after PO and GSL IC usually happens after Dispatch or around Packing Lets say after D RP happens after IC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Frequencies RO Exactly 1 per order CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC Usually 1 per order RP Could be 0 or 1 Lets assume 1 for a completed process skeleton

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Equivalence Do any activities always happen together the same number of times Maybe PO and GSL Every packed order needs a label

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before If CA occurs RO must have occurred If RI occurs CA must have occurred If PI occurs RI must have occurred If QC occurs PI must have occurred If PO occurs QC must have occurred If D occurs PO must have occurred If IC occurs D must have occurred If RP occurs IC must have occurred

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After If RO occurs CA must follow If CA occurs RI must follow and so on

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction In declarative mining Always After usually means If A happens B must happen later Always Before means If B happens A must have happened earlier They are often symmetric in a strict linear process I will populate both to show the bidirectional dependency where applicable

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Never Together In this specific linear flow there are not obvious mutually exclusive paths I will define never together as an empty set because the provided activity list describes a single coherent flow without branching alternatives visible in the text

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Activity Occurrences RO 1 CA 1 RI 1 PI 1 QC 1 PO 1 GSL 1 D 1 IC 1 RP 0 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly Follows RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Definitions always before If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously Example CA RO is correct always after If the first activity occurs then the second activity is executed in one of the following events Example RO CA is correct directly follows RO CA

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait one detail on never together Never Together implies they cannot exist in the same case at all Since all activities belong to the same order fulfillment case never together should logically be empty

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refining equivalence Does PO always equal GSL Yes logically I will add PO GSL as a specific business rule

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Check Keys equivalence always before always after never together activ freq directly follows Values Sets of tuples for constraints Dict of Sets for freq Looks good
